---
title: Tutorials
sidebar: tutorials
---

The tutorials below are executable Jupyter notebooks demonstrating PaleoBeasts
models and utilities. Each notebook can be run locally after
[installing the package](../get-started/installation.qmd).

## Model demos

| Notebook | Model | What it shows |
|---|---|---|
| [Lorenz 63](../../notebooks/model_demos/lorenz63.ipynb) | `Lorenz63` | Chaotic attractor, sensitivity to initial conditions, strange attractor visualization |
| [Lorenz 96](../../notebooks/model_demos/lorenz96.ipynb) | `Lorenz96` | Single-scale and two-scale atmospheric emulator, multi-variable output |
| [EBM](../../notebooks/model_demos/ebm_demo.ipynb) | `EBM` | Zero-dimensional energy balance model, ice-albedo feedback, multiple steady states |
| [Ganopolski 2024](../../notebooks/model_demos/Ganopolski2024_demo.ipynb) | `Model3` | Glacial cycle emulation, orbital forcing, ice volume variability |

## Functionality demos

| Notebook | What it shows |
|---|---|
| [Noise](../../notebooks/functionality_demos/noise_demo.ipynb) | Generating colored noise and AR(1) surrogates |
| [Model noise](../../notebooks/functionality_demos/model_noise_demo.ipynb) | Adding stochastic noise to model output |
| [Downsampling](../../notebooks/functionality_demos/downsample_demo.ipynb) | Simulating irregular sampling from a model trajectory |
